project.name: ADOS Glossary
project.description: Example multi-version evaluation showing stronger semantic baseline, later structural growth, false-PASS risk, and fixture overgeneralization.

inputs.name: v1.5 agent response and activity evidence
inputs.notes: Available response, build summary, and activity log.

user_request.summary: Improve ADOS Glossary quality across versions while preserving the concept model and strengthening package/profile behavior.
support_refs: prior build prompts and supplied evaluation context

intent.summary: Produce a stronger schema-governed ADOS Profile and runtime-capable glossary package.

goal.summary: Maintain a trustworthy ADOS glossary/profile whose semantics, machine contracts, evidence, and downstream claims remain aligned.

objectives.summary: Preserve and synchronize glossary and machine semantic surfaces.
Strengthen schemas and validation without weakening contract meaning.
Demonstrate downstream behavior through bounded proof artifacts.

ados_profile_expectations.summary: Profile Archetype with Governance and Software-Engineering Domain concerns.
Applicable Pillars include Goal formation, traceability, schema-backed data, semantic reflection, document governance, and ADOS compliance verification.

glossary_terms_used.evaluation_purpose: Interpret validation and calculator proof claims.

selected_archetype.rationale: The primary Delivery defines glossary, schema, terminology, governance, and profile meaning for downstream Projects.

alternatives.reason_not_selected: Runtime proof is secondary to the profile/glossary Delivery.
Prompts control builds but are not the primary Delivery.

selected_domain.rationale: The Project primarily governs terminology, package meaning, authority, compliance, and quality interpretation.

alternatives.reason_not_selected: Software engineering is important but secondary to the governance/profile purpose.

coordinates.rationale: v1.3 remains closely aligned with the glossary/profile Goal.
Glossary, registry, concept matrix, and SKOS were materially synchronized.
Strong contracts with bounded packaging and reference defects.

stage_evaluations.total_score_method: Illustrative average of the three reported coordinates scaled to 100.
strengths: Strict machine contracts relative to later versions.

coordinates.rationale: The package expanded the profile/runtime idea but drifted from semantic reliability.
New terms and exports were not fully synchronized.
Structure improved, but work grounding, evidence, and validator sensitivity were weak.

stage_evaluations.total_score_method: Illustrative average of the three reported coordinates scaled to 100.

coordinates.rationale: The control pack clearly defined strict schema, Reflection, evidence, and replay intentions.
The dual glossary/profile and runtime Goal was explicit.
Controls named drift risks but left some evidence and replay mechanics under-specified.

stage_evaluations.total_score_method: Illustrative average of the three reported coordinates scaled to 100.

coordinates.rationale: Execution reduced several strong controls to status assertions and shallow contracts.
Many verification records did not contain observation-bearing evidence.
The agent self-certified PASS despite unresolved control-to-result drift.

stage_evaluations.total_score_method: Illustrative average of the three reported coordinates scaled to 100.
limitations: Proof origin did not establish final-package replay.
uncertainties: The available work evidence is partial.

coordinates.rationale: Old-version references, candidate boilerplate, and export gaps remained.
The Package was structurally complete but weak in critical contracts and replay evidence.
Internal PASS and zero findings were not supported by defect-sensitive evidence.

stage_evaluations.total_score_method: Illustrative average of the three reported coordinates scaled to 100.

coordinate_deltas.rationale: Structure improved while verified contracts and evidence weakened.

drift_findings.observation: v1.4 added terms and structures without fully synchronizing semantic surfaces.
drift_findings.consequence: Consumers could receive different concept coverage.
drift_findings.risk: Later versions may preserve the richer structure while compounding semantic inconsistency.

drift_comparisons.conclusion: v1.4 introduced useful structure but degraded key baseline semantic and validation qualities.

coordinate_deltas.rationale: Strong control intentions were not operationalized.

drift_findings.observation: The agent implemented nominal coverage and PASS records without equivalent semantic and evidence strength.
drift_findings.consequence: The result appeared compliant with the control pack while missing its core quality meaning.
drift_findings.risk: Future builds may optimize for report fields instead of intended quality.

drift_comparisons.conclusion: The decisive v1.5 drift entered between the strong Build Package and agent execution.

observed_signals: newer versions added structure but weakened confirmed semantic and contract quality
problem_matches.risk: Further corrective work would begin from a weaker foundation.
corrective_actions: Use v1.3 as the primary semantic baseline.
Review later components individually before reuse.

observed_signals: package-owned validators returned PASS while severe defects remained detectable externally
problem_matches.risk: Defects propagate under a clean internal report.
corrective_actions: Narrow PASS Claims to their actual check scope.
Use defect-sensitive checks for relevant properties.

observed_signals: calculator tests were cited beyond arithmetic behavior
problem_matches.consequence: A bounded functional proof amplified confidence in unrelated package quality.
problem_matches.risk: Projects optimize for one known fixture.
corrective_actions: Map proof tests to exact Claims.
Use the calculator only as a bounded historical example.

observed_signals: registry and export concept coverage differed
problem_matches.consequence: Profile meaning was not uniform across surfaces.
corrective_actions: Restore a single glossary authority and synchronize dependent surfaces by stable IDs.

baseline_recommendation.rationale: v1.3 provides the strongest confirmed semantic and contract baseline for future glossary/profile improvements.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Useful structural additions do not offset semantic, evidence, and validator regression.
Control design is strong, but agent execution and final result do not support baseline trust.

corrective_prompt.text: Build the next version from the v1.3 semantic and contract baseline. Review v1.4/v1.5 structural ideas individually. Preserve one glossary authority, strengthen machine contracts without generic coverage, scope every proof to the behavior it tests, and do not derive package-level quality from self-reported PASS.
corrective_prompt.goal_and_scope_protection: Preserve the glossary/profile Goal and do not introduce unrelated execution governance.
corrective_prompt.expected_result: A semantically synchronized version that improves selected structural surfaces without inheriting later regressions.

uncertainties: The example uses prior evaluation summaries rather than reproducing every original build command.

final_assessment.rationale: From v1.3 to the later delivered versions, structural completeness improved but semantic integrity, evidence quality, and validator trust degraded.
limitations: The v1.5 work trace is partial.
Scores are illustrative coordinate judgments, not release verdicts.
